data.title: Bitcoin Price Constrained by Whale Orders, Says CoinGlass
data.description:
On April 3, CoinGlass reported that Bitcoin's price is currently confined within a specific range due to whale order book data. According to BlockBeats, sell orders are concentrated between $67,500 and the $67,950 to $68,050 range, while buy orders are positioned between $65,600 and $65,800, with strong support near $64,900.

The market is experiencing fluctuations rather than a trend. If the sell wall is absorbed, the outlook may become bullish; conversely, if the buy orders are withdrawn or consumed, the sentiment could turn bearish. Until then, the price remains compressed within the range set by the whales.

data.title: Turkey's Inflation Rate Rises 30.87% in March, Slightly Below Expectations
data.description: Turkey's consumer prices increased by 30.87% year-on-year in March, according to Jin10. This figure is slightly below market expectations, which anticipated a rise of 31.40%. The inflation rate reflects ongoing economic challenges in the country, as policymakers continue to address the impacts of rising costs on the economy.

data.title: Türkiye sold $20 billion worth of gold in three weeks to support its lira.
data.description: Türkiye has sold approximately 120 tons of gold, worth $20 billion, in three weeks to support its lira, including about 70 tons last week. (Solid Intel)

data.title: South Korea Orders Dunamu to Amend Disclosure on Stock Swap with Naver Financial
data.description: South Korea's Financial Supervisory Service has instructed Dunamu to revise its public disclosure regarding a comprehensive stock swap and transfer with Naver Financial. According to NS3.AI, the regulator highlighted that Dunamu's report contained significant omissions or false statements concerning future corporate restructuring plans and other matters crucial to investment decisions. In November, Dunamu announced that the plan would result in it becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Naver Financial, with current shareholders receiving shares in Naver Financial.

data.title: CZ's new book, *Freedom of Money*, has become the number one bestseller in the crypto category on Amazon.
data.description: Amazon's website shows that CZ's new book, *Freedom of Money*, has become the number one bestseller in the crypto category. The book is priced at $9.99, and readers who pre-order will receive the digital version on April 8th. Previously, CZ stated that all proceeds from the book's sales will be donated to charity.

data.title: France's February Industrial Output Declines by 0.3%
data.description: France's industrial production in February showed a year-on-year decline of 0.3%, according to Jin10. This figure fell short of the expected 1.6% growth and was a decrease from the previous value of 2.4%. The data indicates a slowdown in the country's industrial sector, raising concerns about economic momentum.

data.title: Lista DAO has launched Dow Protocol E-Commerce Financing RWA Vault; APY is currently at 10%.
data.description: Lista DAO launched the Dow E-Commerce Financing RWA Vault on Dow Protocol today. This Vault is an RWA platform built on the capabilities of HSBC's Dowsure e-commerce finance company, with a 90-day term and a current APY of 10%. The Vault provides cash flow support to merchants based on their accounts receivable and repayment cash flow from established global leading e-commerce platforms. Merchant repayment discipline is reportedly guaranteed by two core mechanisms: first, an automated repayment service routing mechanism in cooperation with the E-Commerce platform, which prioritizes the collection of merchant repayments and automatically deducts principal and interest as agreed; second, a dual-lock account control mechanism, which restricts changes to repayment paths, verifies control of repayment accounts, and supports corresponding protective measures in the event of overdue payments or defaults. This Vault does not represent a purely on-chain incentive structure, but rather a cash flow system that has been operating and proven in the real-world business world for a long time.

data.title: Mercor Faces Major Security Breach Affecting AI Companies
data.description:
A significant security breach has impacted Mercor, a startup providing training data to AI companies such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta. According to Fortune and ChainCatcher, the incident resulted from a supply chain attack on the open-source library LiteLLM, widely used by developers to connect AI services, with millions of daily downloads.

The attack was initiated by the hacker group TeamPCP, which inserted malicious code into LiteLLM to steal credentials. Subsequently, another hacker group, Lapsus$, claimed to have obtained up to 4TB of Mercor's data, including source code, database records, internal Slack communications, and platform conversation videos. Unverified reports suggest that some customer datasets and confidential AI project information may have been compromised.

Mercor has responded swiftly to contain the situation and has launched a third-party forensic investigation to address the breach.

data.title: Dmail Network will be gradually shutting down starting May 15th; users need to export their emails before then.
data.description: According to an official announcement, the decentralized email project Dmail Network will gradually cease all services starting May 15, 2026. Users must export their email content and apply to cancel their accounts before this date; all data will be completely deleted afterward. Dmail stated that due to factors such as the high cost of decentralized infrastructure, the failure to establish a viable commercialization path, the lack of practical use cases for its tokens, and the loss of core team members, the project could not achieve healthy and sustainable operation.

data.title: Global Markets Face Structural Challenges Amid Inflation and Policy Constraints
data.description:
On April 3, the market's core issues have shifted from purely war-related risks to a structural impasse characterized by persistent inflation and policy constraints. According to BlockBeats, energy concerns remain unresolved, with Russian exports affected and OPEC+ potentially increasing output. The U.S. has not discussed releasing reserves, and the uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz continues to keep oil and diesel prices high. Additionally, tariffs on steel, aluminum, and potential pharmaceutical tariffs indicate that cost pressures are spreading from energy to broader manufacturing and consumer sectors, with global supply chain pressures persisting and even expanding.

On the policy front, liquidity support is confirmed to be unavailable. Key Federal Reserve officials are inclined to maintain the status quo, opting to adjust liquidity structures through regulatory tools rather than rate cuts. The IMF has pointed out that there is almost no room for rate cuts in the coming year, indicating a systemic revision of market expectations for policy easing. With inflation driven by energy and employment not yet significantly deteriorating, monetary policy remains constrained, lacking conditions for asset valuation expansion.

In this context, financial behavior is becoming more conservative and short-term focused. Changes in gold reserves and the divergence in fundamentals between technology and traditional industry companies are evident. Bitcoin remains in a passive pricing state, unable to effectively break through the liquidity pressure zone around 69,400, indicating insufficient buying momentum. Meanwhile, liquidity continues to accumulate near 65,500, which could become a major release point if macro pressures intensify.

Overall, the market has entered a "high inflation expectations + policy constraints + war expansion" environment. With liquidity unable to ease, supply chains disrupted, and geopolitical risks lacking resolution, price fluctuations increasingly reflect capital redistribution. In the short term, the market is expected to remain in an unstable equilibrium.

data.title: NFT Projects Focus on IP and Utility Amid Declining Speculative Demand
data.description: NFT projects are increasingly emphasizing intellectual property (IP) and utility as speculative demand diminishes. According to NS3.AI, CoinDesk Research reported over $13 million in retail sales for Pudgy Penguins, highlighting a shift in the market. Industry executives note that scarcity alone is insufficient to maintain value, urging projects to develop robust business models, achieve product-market fit, and establish sustainable use cases to remain competitive.

data.title: Jupiter's Trading Volume Surges Amid Drift Theft Incident
data.description:
On April 3, Jupiter's trading volume experienced a significant increase following the ongoing developments surrounding the Drift theft incident. According to BlockBeats, the situation was further influenced by Jupiter's official statement that the platform was not affected by the event. This led to some funds potentially exiting through Jupiter, causing a 3.1% rise in JUP, with its peak briefly surpassing $0.16.

During this period, a major whale with the address starting 0x8b0 on Hyperliquid closed a JUP short position within half an hour, incurring a loss of approximately $33,700. The closing average price was $0.1625, with the position previously valued at $1.22 million, marking it as the largest short position on the platform.

data.title: Ethereum's Market Share in Non-USD Stablecoins Declines
data.description:
Ethereum's dominance in the non-USD stablecoin market has decreased significantly, according to recent data from Dune and Visa. At the beginning of 2023, Ethereum held a 90% market share, which has dropped to 65% by February 2026, indicating that other blockchains are rapidly gaining ground. Despite this decline, Ethereum remains the primary blockchain for stablecoin issuance.

According to ChainCatcher, Ethereum ranks fifth in terms of the number of independent senders within the stablecoin network. However, the overall user adoption within its ecosystem continues to grow. The number of independent senders has surged from 2,000 last year to 12,000, reflecting a rapid expansion of the stablecoin ecosystem.

data.title: Amazon's $9 Billion Acquisition of Globalstar Signals Strategic Shift in Satellite Industry
data.description: Amazon plans to acquire Globalstar for $9 billion, marking a significant move in the satellite industry. According to PANews, this acquisition is not just a typical merger but highlights the transition of low-Earth orbit satellite competition into a phase of resource integration. The article examines Amazon's strategic efforts to catch up with Starlink, focusing on spectrum resources, industry dynamics, and ecosystem competition. Additionally, it explores Apple's pivotal role in this evolving landscape. The low-Earth orbit satellite sector is shifting from a space technology race to a battle over global communication infrastructure and future network gateways.

data.title: XPL Token Experiences Significant Price Fluctuations
data.description:
The price of XPL token experienced significant volatility on April 3, according to BlockBeats. Monitoring data from Coinglass and Hyperinsight revealed that between 1 a.m. and 5 a.m. (UTC+8), the token's price surged by 42%, reaching a peak of $0.167. However, starting at 5:10 a.m., the price plummeted by over 40% in less than five minutes, falling below $0.12 and erasing all gains made earlier in the day.

Before the price surge, five new addresses on Hyperliquid deposited funds around 1 a.m., each transaction exceeding $200,000, totaling approximately $1.292 million. These addresses opened high-leverage long positions on XPL, possibly in coordination with spot purchases on other platforms to drive up the price. During this period, the five addresses saw substantial floating profits, with their initial investments more than doubling.

Between 4:30 and 4:40 a.m., these addresses withdrew their released margins along with the floating profits, significantly raising the liquidation price of their positions. At 5:10 a.m., the five addresses were liquidated simultaneously at an XPL price of approximately $0.12971, causing the price to drop sharply, possibly in coordination with spot market sell-offs. The combined profit for these addresses was around $1.602 million.

During the price surge, several large short positions were liquidated, including those held by addresses (0xbe1), (0x51f), "美布两油主力空军" (0x985), and (0x45d), with the total size of liquidated short positions exceeding $32.99 million.

The source of funds was traced to the on-chain address: 0xBc1D9760bd6ca468CA9fB5Ff2CFbEAC35d86c973. The suspected whale addresses involved in the operation are:

1. 0x0e321565595044b32784fc983d38bfe57ddee62c

2. 0x763a5940ccb568e594a9ceb4b1d88d60dec8ea08

3. 0x1b546e2a2f10d30245502d6bdb11bba23719597f

4. 0x1a1cc7a769b233f29e09050fe383a37c46bcdbdf

5. 0xfb8fe47311c92345b8fc152f697212595a778915

data.title: Drift Protocol Reaches Out to Wallet Holding Stolen ETH
data.description: Drift Protocol has sent an on-chain message to the wallet holding stolen ETH. According to Foresight News, the official statement indicates that the attacker can contact the team via Blockscan chat.

data.title: Drift: Key information regarding the parties involved in the vulnerability attack has been confirmed, and on-chain messages are being sent to wallets holding stolen funds.
data.description: Drift announced on the X platform that it has confirmed key information regarding the parties involved in the vulnerability attack. Drift is currently sending on-chain messages from the address (0x093...C105) to the four ETH wallets holding the stolen funds, indicating its readiness to engage in dialogue and requesting them to contact it via Blockscan chat. Drift will share further updates with the community after third-party attribution is completed. Previously, it was reported that the Solana ecosystem's Drift Protocol was attacked, resulting in losses of at least $200 million.

data.description:
1. CZ: New book "Freedom of Money" will be released next week; ebook pre-orders are now open.
2. Bitcoin mining company Riot sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 2026.
3. Trump says the new Iranian regime must act quickly.
4. CZ: All proceeds from the sale of his new book "Freedom of Money" will be donated to charity.

6. Intergaze will officially shut down; users must complete asset withdrawals within 14 days before the bridge closes.
7. A whale sold 1,323 ETH after holding them for two months, incurring a loss of $1.28 million.

data.title: Polymarket's Revenue Ranks Fifth Among Crypto Protocols
data.description:
Polymarket has achieved a notable position in the crypto sector, ranking fifth in daily revenue among crypto protocols, with earnings reaching $1.71 million, according to BlockBeats. This development follows the introduction of a new fee structure on March 30, which imposes Taker fees across nearly all trading categories for the first time.

The new fee system features variable rates, with peak fees for cryptocurrency-related contracts reaching up to 1.8%. Actual fees fluctuate based on share prices and market dynamics. Categories such as sports, finance, politics, culture, weather, and general predictions have lower tiered rates, while certain economic predictions and mention-based categories have higher peak rates, approximately 1.5%.

In response to community inquiries about whether the platform will support POLY token payments for fee discounts, Polymarket team member Mustafa confirmed that this option will be available.

data.title: NYSE and Nasdaq Closed for Good Friday, Trading to Resume April 6
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and Nasdaq were closed on April 3 in observance of Good Friday, marking one of Nasdaq's ten full-day closures in 2026. According to NS3.AI, while the stock exchanges remained closed, banks continued their operations. Stock trading is scheduled to resume on Monday, April 6, at 9:30 a.m. ET.
